display.purpose: The hero name at the top of the page. Appears exactly once.

title.purpose: Project names, writing titles. Never larger than 1.125rem.

body.purpose: About paragraphs, writing excerpts, social handles. Line-length capped near 65ch.

label.purpose: Section eyebrows (About, Selected Work, Writing, Elsewhere) and the availability pill.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Ease-out-quart for the only live transitions in the system: hover underline fades and nav backdrop tint. No layout animation, no bounce.

motion.name: duration-quick
motion.purpose: Hover state changes (underline appearance). Fast enough to feel instant.

components.name: Availability Pill
components.description: The one chromatic object on the page: an ink-filled pill with a paper-colored status dot and uppercase mono copy. Signals that the person is available.
components.css: .ds-availability-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #1a1a1a; color: #faf8f3; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0; text-transform: none; } .ds-availability-pill__dot { display: inline-block; width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #faf8f3; margin-right: 8px; }

components.name: Section Rubric
components.description: The monospace uppercase eyebrow that opens every section. The system's only section-break device.
components.css: .ds-rubric { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b6b6b; margin: 0; }

components.name: Project Tile
components.description: A 4:3 solid color block with a typographic caption below. The color IS the artwork; no images, no gradients.
components.html:
Project Name — a short description of the work

Lead Designer · 2024
components.css: .ds-project-tile { display: block; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; } .ds-project-tile__block { aspect-ratio: 4 / 3; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(26,26,26,0.05); } .ds-project-tile__caption { margin-top: 16px; } .ds-project-tile__title { font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0; } .ds-project-tile:hover .ds-project-tile__title { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-project-tile__desc { color: #6b6b6b; font-weight: 400; } .ds-project-tile__meta { font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6b6b6b; margin: 8px 0 0; }

components.name: Writing Entry
components.description: An unstyled editorial list row: title, date, excerpt. No card, no container.
components.html:
On Reading Slowly

March 2024

A short meditation on the practice of slow reading, and what the printed page still teaches us in an age of scroll.
components.css: .ds-writing { display: block; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; } .ds-writing__title { font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0; } .ds-writing:hover .ds-writing__title { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-writing__date { font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6b6b6b; margin: 4px 0 0; } .ds-writing__excerpt { font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.625; color: #6b6b6b; margin: 8px 0 0; }

components.name: Nav Bar
components.description: Sticky top bar with the display-serif name on the left and two small sans links on the right. The only element in the system that uses backdrop blur.
components.html:
Mia Bergström
WorkWriting
components.css: .ds-nav { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10; background: rgba(250,248,243,0.8); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(4px); border-bottom: 1px solid #e8e4d9; } .ds-nav__inner { max-width: 672px; margin: 0 auto; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 16px 24px; } .ds-nav__brand { font-family: 'Instrument Serif', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.125rem; color: #1a1a1a; } .ds-nav__links { display: flex; gap: 24px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-nav__links a { color: #1a1a1a; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav__links a:hover, .ds-nav__links a:focus-visible { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-nav__links a:focus-visible { outline: none; }

components.name: Footer Social Link
components.description: A label + handle pair on one baseline. The label stays muted; the handle underlines on hover.
components.css: .ds-social { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 12px; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-social__label { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6b6b6b; } .ds-social__handle { font-size: 1rem; color: #1a1a1a; } .ds-social:hover .ds-social__handle { text-decoration: underline; }

narrative.overview:
This system behaves like a short editorial monograph set on warm uncoated paper. A single narrow column of type runs down the middle of a cream field. Display type is a high-contrast serif; body is a quiet humanist sans; labels are a uniform-width monospace in full uppercase, spaced wide like a chapter mark. There is exactly one page of content, read top to bottom, and nothing competes with the type. The only chromatic object on the page is a single ink-colored availability pill. Everything else is paper, ink, and rule.

The system rejects the vocabulary of product-marketing pages. No hero gradient, no card grid of feature tiles, no glassmorphism, no pastel illustrations, no CTA stack. Density is low and rhythm is even: sections are separated by 96px of air, and the column never widens beyond ~640px so body lines stay comfortably under 75ch. Depth is conveyed entirely by typographic weight and a single 1px rule; there are no shadows in the system.
keyCharacteristics: One narrow measure (max-width 672px / 42rem) holds every reading surface.
Type-driven hierarchy: one display serif, one body sans, one mono used only for labels.
Flat by doctrine: no shadows, no elevation, no tonal layering.
Restrained color strategy: two neutrals, one ink, one rule — no accent hue.
Section rubrics are monospace uppercase with wide tracking, never sentence case.

rules.name: The Two-Neutrals Rule
rules.body: The system ships with paper + ink + one recession step (ink-muted) and one rule color. No accent hue, no chart colors, no gradients. If a new surface 'needs' a color to work, the design needs rework, not a color.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward the paper hue (~85° chroma 0.003–0.012). Pure #000 and pure #fff are forbidden; they read as screen-native and break the printed-page illusion.

rules.name: The Mono Rubric Rule
rules.body: Every section opens with a monospace, uppercase, wide-tracked label in ink-muted. Never a larger sans headline. The rubric is the section break.

rules.name: The One Display Rule
rules.body: The serif display size appears once, on the hero name. Everything downstream uses the sans title size at most.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No box-shadow declarations exist in the system. If a surface needs visual separation, use the rule color as a 1px line or add typographic whitespace.

rules.name: The One Blur Rule
rules.body: The sticky nav is the only element permitted to use backdrop-filter. Blur is a functional legibility tool here, not a decorative treatment.

rules.name: The No-Card Rule
rules.body: Writing entries, About paragraphs, and footer links never live inside cards. The column itself is the container. Only the project tile wears a visual frame.

rules.name: The Hover Underline Rule
rules.body: Every link target in the system reveals itself by underline on hover. No color shifts, no icon reveals, no background fills.

dos: Do keep the reading column at max-w-2xl (672px) and add air between sections with space-y-24 (96px). Rhythm comes from spacing, not rules.
Do open every section with a monospace uppercase tracking-widest rubric in text-ink-muted.
Do use solid colors for project tiles. The handle app cannot edit gradients yet: a single backgroundColor keeps the tile editable.
Do tint neutrals toward the paper hue (warm yellow, ~85°, chroma ≤0.012). Paper is #faf8f3, not #ffffff.
Do reserve the display serif size for the hero name only. One display size per page.
Do signal links with group-hover:underline, not color change.
donts: Don't introduce an accent color. The palette is two neutrals plus ink.
Don't reach for box-shadow. This system is flat by doctrine. Use a 1px rule or whitespace.
Don't add nested cards. The project tile is the only framed element.
Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. They read as screen-native and break the cream-paper world.
Don't apply gradients, neither to tiles (the handle app can't edit them yet) nor to text (background-clip: text is banned outright).
Don't replace the mono rubric with a larger sans or serif headline. The rubric is the section break.
Don't widen the column for 'more space to work with.' The narrow measure is the design.
Don't add border-left or border-right stripes as decorative accents on list items or callouts.
